info.description:
Core backend service for BookEat.

Every response is wrapped in a JSON envelope — {"data": ...} on
success, {"error": "..."} on failure — except /.well-known/jwks.json,
which returns a raw JWKS document.
info.title: BookEat backend-core API

get.description:
Public RS256 keys used to verify access-token signatures. This
response is NOT wrapped in the standard envelope.

post.description: Authenticates by email + password and returns a token pair.
Revokes the given refresh token so it can no longer be used.

Generates a one-time code and delivers it to the phone. Rate-limited
(per-minute and per-hour); over the limit returns 422. The response
"code" field is populated only when AUTH_OTP_DEV_EXPOSE=true.

Verifies the latest active code for the phone. On success, finds or
creates the user and returns a token pair. Wrong/expired codes and
too many attempts return 401.
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair.
Registers a new user with email + password and returns a token pair.

get.description: Returns the booking's live (authorized or captured) payment. Same access rule as reading the payment directly.

post.description: Computes the amount server-side, places a hold with the resolved acquirer, and returns a payment link. Works for a logged-in guest, an anonymous guest checkout link, or venue staff creating a payment on a guest's behalf.

parameters.description: Where the guest lands after the hosted payment page
parameters.name: body

409.description: booking already has an active payment

422.description: validation failed / payments not enabled for this restaurant

post.description: Venue staff only. Idempotent: calling it again on an already-captured payment is a no-op.
Refunds the guest, keeps the venue's share, or both, depending on the trigger and timing (spec §9.1).
post.summary: Settle a cancelled or no-show booking's payment

post.description: Venue staff only. Idempotent: calling it again on an already-voided payment is a no-op.

put.summary: Add a restaurant to my favorites

delete.summary: Remove a restaurant from my favorites

get.description: The payment's own owner, venue staff/admin of its restaurant, or (for a guest checkout with no account) anyone holding the id may read it.
Returns the profile of the authenticated user.

delete.description:
Soft-deletes and anonymizes the authenticated user's account.
Bookings/payments keep their reference to the (now anonymized)
user id; they are never deleted or altered. Idempotent.

patch.description:
Partially updates the authenticated user's profile. Only the
provided fields are changed; omitted fields are left untouched.

get.description: Returns 200 while the process is up. Does not check dependencies.
Pings the database. Returns 200 when reachable, 503 otherwise.

post.description: Public, unauthenticated — authenticity is the pg_sig signature, verified server-side. Answers a signed XML envelope, never JSON. A bad signature or a processing failure both answer pg_status=error with no further detail, which makes FreedomPay retry every 30 minutes for up to 2 hours (sandbox-confirmed); a successful apply answers pg_status=ok.
Public, unauthenticated — authenticity is the Content-HMAC/X-Content-HMAC signature, verified server-side. Always answers {"code":0}, the only value TipTopPay's docs describe as "registered".

parameters.description: check | pay | fail | confirm | refund | cancel
parameters.name: type

payment_url.description:
PaymentURL is where the guest is redirected to actually pay. Present
once the acquirer answered Authorize; nil for a payment that never got
that far (should not normally be observable — CreateForBooking only
ever stores a row after a successful Authorize).

manual_cancelled_at.description:
ManualCancelledAt overrides the booking's own recorded cancellation time.
STAFF/ADMIN ONLY — the usecase itself rejects it from any other actor
(SettleInput.ManualCancelledAt's doc comment); accepting the field here
and letting the usecase reject it is simpler and no less safe than
trying to strip it in the handler before the actor's role is known.

country_code.description: CountryCode is an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code, e.g. "KZ".

cuisine_category_ids.description:
CuisineCategoryIDs replaces the foodie profile's picked cuisines when
present (including an empty array, which clears all picks). Omit the
field entirely to leave existing picks unchanged.

CuisineCategoryIDs is the foodie profile: ids from the restaurants' own
category dictionary (restaurant_categories) the user picked.

BearerAuth.description:
Access token from POST /api/v1/auth/login (or signup / otp/verify /
refresh), sent as: Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
